data.content:
# Implementation Plan: Extend scope refinement timeout to 10m with agent retry fallback

## Summary

Three changes: bump the timeout constant, make the prompt runner accept an externally-resolved agent, and teach the worker to track failed agents per-beat so retries prefer a different pool member.

## Complexity: Low-Medium

- 3 source files changed, 1 test file updated
- All pool-exclusion infrastructure already exists (selectFromPool with excludeAgentId)
- No schema or settings changes needed

## Step 1 — Increase timeout (scope-refinement-prompt.ts)

File: src/lib/scope-refinement-prompt.ts:15

Change PROMPT_TIMEOUT_MS from 180_000 to 600_000 (3 min → 10 min).

## Step 2 — Accept pre-resolved agent in prompt runner (scope-refinement-prompt.ts)

File: src/lib/scope-refinement-prompt.ts — runScopeRefinementPrompt

Currently runScopeRefinementPrompt resolves the agent internally via getScopeRefinementAgent(). The worker also resolves the agent at the top of processScopeRefinementJob (line 157) to guard against "no agent configured". This double-resolve prevents the caller from controlling which agent is used.

Change: Add an agent: AgentTarget parameter to runScopeRefinementPrompt so the caller passes in the already-resolved agent. Remove the internal getScopeRefinementAgent() call and the null guard (the caller handles that).

## Step 3 — Add getScopeRefinementAgentExcluding (settings.ts)

File: src/lib/settings.ts

Add a new exported function:

  export async function getScopeRefinementAgentExcluding(
    excludeIds: ReadonlySet<string>,
  ): Promise<AgentTarget | null>

Logic:
- In advanced (pool) mode: call resolvePoolAgent("scope_refinement", pools, agents, excludeIds) — the existing selectFromPool already handles exclusion sets.
- In basic mode: check if the single configured agent's ID is in the exclusion set; if so, return null.

## Step 4 — Track failed agents and use different agent on retry (scope-refinement-worker.ts)

File: src/lib/scope-refinement-worker.ts

### 4a. Extend WorkerState

Add failedAgents: Map<string, Set<string>> — keyed by beatId, value is the set of agent IDs that have failed for that beat.

### 4b. Modify processScopeRefinementJob

Current flow resolves agent then calls runScopeRefinementPrompt. New flow:

1. Look up failedAgents.get(job.beatId) for the exclusion set.
2. If exclusion set is non-empty, call getScopeRefinementAgentExcluding(excludeIds).
3. If that returns null (no alternative), fall back to getScopeRefinementAgent() (re-use any agent rather than giving up entirely).
4. If still null, skip with "no agent configured" (existing behavior).
5. Pass the resolved agent to runScopeRefinementPrompt(prompt, repoPath, agent).
6. On success: delete failedAgents entry for the beat.
7. On failure: record the agent's agentId in the failedAgents set before calling maybeReenqueue.

### 4c. Clean up state

- resetScopeRefinementWorkerState clears failedAgents.
- maybeReenqueue already cleans retryCounts on max-retries — also clean failedAgents there.

## Step 5 — Update tests (scope-refinement-worker.test.ts)

File: src/lib/__tests__/scope-refinement-worker.test.ts

1. Timeout test (line 275): Change vi.advanceTimersByTime(180_001) → vi.advanceTimersByTime(600_001) and update the test description.
2. New test: "retries with different agent when pool has alternatives" — mock getScopeRefinementAgentExcluding to return a second agent, verify the second attempt uses the alternative.
3. New test: "falls back to original agent when no alternative available" — mock exclusion-based resolution to return null, verify fallback to getScopeRefinementAgent.
4. New test: "fails with descriptive error when all retries exhausted" — verify recentFailures message mentions agent exhaustion.

## Risks

- Longer timeout means workers are occupied longer on slow agents, reducing throughput. Mitigated by the existing MAX_WORKERS = 2 concurrency — worst case is 2 workers blocked for 10 min each instead of 3 min.
- Basic mode (non-pool, single agent) cannot provide alternatives — retry will reuse the same agent. This is acceptable and matches current behavior.

## Dependencies

None. All required pool-exclusion infrastructure exists in agent-pool.ts.
